channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Police say a person is in custody after a suspected arson fire at Pennsylvania governor's mansion
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — Police said Sunday a man was arrested and will face charges including attempted murder, terrorism and attempted arson in an early morning fire that badly damaged the Pennsylvania governor's mansion and forced Gov. Josh Shapiro, his family and guests to quickly escape.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US still won't say whether it will return mistakenly deported man, despite Supreme Court decision
item.description: The Trump administration is doubling down on its decision not to tell a federal court whether it has any plans to repatriate a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported last month and remains confined in a notorious prison in El Salvador, despite a Supreme Court ruling and lower court order that the
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: 6 injured after explosion, house collapse in Austin
item.description: One house was "leveled" while another "suffered severe collapse damage" following an explosion in Austin on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Julianna Russ, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's commerce secretary says new electronics tariff exemptions are temporary, chip tariffs coming
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Tariff exemptions announced Friday on electronics like smartphones and laptops are only a temporary reprieve until the Trump administration develops a new tariff approach specific to the semiconductor industry, U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: MAE ANDERSON and MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 major unreleased movies we'll (probably) never get to see
item.description: "Coyote vs. Acme" was recently rescued from obscurity after being shelved by Warner Bros. Discovery. But other films haven't been so lucky.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: 6 injured after house explosion in northwest Austin, cause under investigation
item.description: Several first responders in northwest Austin were at the scene of a house collapse Sunday after several callers heard a loud explosion in the area.
item.dc:creator: Julianna Russ, NewsNation

item.title: Trump is 'fully fit' to serve as commander in chief, his doctor says after recent physical
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Donald Trump’s doctor says the oldest man elected president is “fully fit” to serve as commander in chief as the White House released results Sunday of Trump's recent physical exam.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Schumer seeks to halt flights for New York helicopter company after deadly Hudson River crash
item.description: New York Sen. Chuck Schumer is calling on federal authorities to revoke the operating permits of the helicopter tour company whose sightseeing chopper broke apart in midair and plunged into the Hudson River.
item.dc:creator: Jake Offenhartz, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Search continues for Las Vegas veterinarian who apologized for kicking horse
item.description: Earlier this month, Dr. Frehner apologized for kicking a horse he was hired to give shots to. Days later, he was reported missing in the Lake Mead Recreation Area.
item.dc:creator: Madina Ansary, KRON4

item.title: Wisconsin teen charged in parents’ deaths is accused of plotting to kill Trump
item.description: A Wisconsin teenager charged in the deaths of his parents faces wider allegations that he killed them to “obtain the financial means” to assassinate President Donald Trump and overthrow the government.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The hierarchy behind the Hawaiian shirts at Trader Joe's
item.description: If you’re shopping at Trader Joe’s and you need the kind of assistance that only a seasoned, veteran TJ’s employee can provide, you might want to look at their shirts.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump tariffs bring Democrat disagreements to surface
item.description: President Donald Trump’s tariffs are putting Democrats in a challenging political position as they decide how strongly to denounce his trade policy.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KRON4

item.title: Takeaways from AP's report on how flat funding set stage for Texas measles outbreak, might fuel more
item.description: The measles outbreak in West Texas didn’t happen just by chance. The easily preventable disease, declared eliminated in the U.S. in 2000, ripped through Texas communities in part because health departments were starved of the funding needed to run vaccine programs, officials say.
item.dc:creator: LAURA UNGAR, MICHELLE R. SMITH and DEVI SHASTRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Understanding mutual funds: A beginner's guide to investing
item.description: Despite recent stock market slumps and fears in the investment world, it's never a bad time to learn about strategies to boost your investing experience.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, KRON4

item.title: Some top tech leaders have embraced Trump. That's created a political divide in Silicon Valley
item.description: Many of Silicon Valley's most prominent tech leaders have embraced President Donald Trump in his second term.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Riccardi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Make-A-Wish grants Masters trip to teen cancer survivor
item.description: Lucas Pacheco, a 18-year-old lymphoma survivor, fulfilled his dream of attending The Masters through the 'Make-A-Wish' Foundation, which granted wishes to children with life-threatening illnesses.
item.dc:creator: Barclay Bishop, Fox 5

item.title: Intellectually disabled teen shot by Idaho police dies after being removed from life support
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — An autistic, nonverbal teenage boy who was shot repeatedly by Idaho police from the other side of a chain-link fence while he was holding a knife died Saturday after being removed from life support, his family said.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE and REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kansas man wrongly accused in Chiefs parade shooting found dead
item.description: An Olathe, Kansas man who was wrongly accused of being a "shooter" and "illegal alien" at the Chiefs Super Bowl rally in 2024 has died.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, WGN 9

item.title: Texas man suing NFL player over December shooting at car lot in Nashville
item.description: A Texas man who says Titans cornerback L'Jarius Sneed or his personal assistant shot at him while he was sitting inside a Mercedes-Benz on a dealer lot in December is suing for at least $1 million.
item.dc:creator: Teresa M. Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: ICE deports former Mexico governor serving federal prison sentence in Illinois
item.description: ICE turned over convicted criminal Tomas Jesus Yarrington Ruvalcaba, former governor of Tamaulipas, Mexico, to Mexican authorities at the San Ysidro Port of Entry.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Woman unknowingly gives birth to a stranger's baby after IVF clinic error
item.description: A woman in Australia unknowingly gave birth to a stranger’s baby after she received another patient's embryo from her in vitro fertilization clinic due to “human error,” the clinic said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Deadly crash raises new questions about safety of New York’s helicopter tours
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A helicopter ride giving a thrilling sweep of Manhattan’s iconic skyline has long been on the to-do list for New York City tourists of means.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Prince Harry makes visit to Ukraine, meets with wounded veterans
item.description: The Duke of Sussex visited the Superhumans Center in Lviv on Thursday, a spokesperson confirmed to ITK.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Strict Idaho abortion ban loosened by judge's ruling on medical exemptions
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — An abortion in Idaho is not prohibited if pregnancy complications could cause a woman's death, even if that death “is neither imminent nor assured,” a state judge said Friday in a ruling that loosens one of the strictest abortion bans in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Columbia University activist Mahmoud Khalil can be deported, immigration judge rules
item.description: JENA, La. (AP) — Columbia University graduate student Mahmoud Khalil can be forced out of the country as a national security risk, an immigration judge in Louisiana ruled Friday after lawyers argued the legality of deporting the activist who participated in pro-Palestinian demonstrations.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: South Carolina executes second man by firing squad in 5 weeks
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A firing squad on Friday executed a South Carolina man who killed an off-duty police officer, the second time the rare execution method has been used by the state in the past five weeks.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Where is crime happening? New map shows recent crimes in Salt Lake neighborhoods
item.description: The Salt Lake City Police Department created an interactive map showing recent crimes across the city.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, ABC 4

item.title: US may already be in recession: BlackRock CEO
item.description: BlackRock CEO Larry Fink said during a recent interview that the United States may already be in a recession or getting very close to one as a result of President Trump’s sweeping tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Divers search for parts after NYC helicopter crash killed a family of five and the pilot, a Navy vet
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Divers using sonar searched Friday for key pieces of a sightseeing helicopter that broke apart in midair and plunged into the Hudson River between Manhattan and New Jersey. All six people aboard were killed — a family of five from Spain and the pilot, a 36-year-old U.S. Navy veteran.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, DAVE COLLINS and TED SHAFFREY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ghislaine Maxwell, Epstein accomplice, appeals case to Supreme Court
item.description: Maxwell was convicted back in December 2021 for helping Jeffrey Epstein sexually exploit and abuse underage girls.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Trump eyes major cuts to NOAA research
item.description: The Trump administration is eyeing cuts to climate, weather and ocean research in a draft budget blueprint for the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA).
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Stocks climb but the dollar sinks as Wall Street closes a chaotic week
item.description: U.S. stocks are climbing in shaky trading Friday as President Donald Trump’s trade war with China escalates further and U.S. households get more worried about it.
item.dc:creator: Stan Choe, ABC 4

item.title: Pressed for evidence against Mahmoud Khalil, government cites its power to deport people for beliefs
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Facing a deadline from an immigration judge to turn over evidence for its attempted deportation of Columbia University activist Mahmoud Khalil, the federal government has instead submitted a brief memo, signed by Secretary of State Marco Rubio, citing the Trump administration’s autho
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Near record heat builds across Utah ahead of cooldown
item.description: The heat is on across the Beehive State for the next few days with cooler weather forecasted to set in Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Alana Brophy, ABC 4

item.title: Stocks slide as Trump's trade war with China spooks Wall Street
item.description: Stocks slid Thursday as President Trump’s escalating trade war with China and concerns about its economic implications continued to rattle Wall Street.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's tariffs threaten to end quarter-century era of cheap goods for U.S. consumers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s new tariffs threaten to push up prices on clothes, mobile phones, furniture and many other products in the coming months, possibly ending the era of cheap goods that Americans enjoyed for about a quarter-century before the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER RUGABER and ANNE D'INNOCENZIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: S&P 500 sinks 3.5% as Wall Street swings from euphoria back to worries about US-China trade war
item.description: The losses for U.S. stocks accelerated Thursday after the White House clarified that Chinese imports will be tariffed at 145%, not the 125% rate that Trump had written about in his posting on Truth Social Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
